# graphobs v0.3.1

Internal consolidation of contract-enforcement and payload-projection code, with no change to the package-root interface.

## Highlights

- Contract conformance now lives in one place: `contracts/conformance.py` owns
  undeclared-read detection, undeclared-write detection, and violation
  reporting, so runtime write validation, runtime read enforcement, and
  sample-based drift checks share one implementation instead of re-deriving the
  same rules.
- The duplicated `state/policies.py` is folded into `state/observed_access.py`:
  write-path authorization (`policy_allows_write_path`) sits beside the read
  check under one `PathPolicy` shape, removing byte-identical helper copies.
- Node and callback payload projection are unified behind a single
  `observe_payload` with an explicit `PayloadObservation` policy
  (`STRICT_OBSERVATION` / `COMPACT_OBSERVATION`), so span input, span output, and
  callback payloads are curated consistently instead of diverging silently. Span
  input is now projected through the same contract policy as span output.

## Migration Notes

These changes are internal refactors and the package-root interface is
unchanged. Code that imports lower-level submodules should note:

- `graphobs.state.policies` is removed. Import `policy_allows_write_path` from
  `graphobs.state.observed_access`; for read checks use
  `policy_allows_observed_read_path` from the same module.
- `graphobs.contracts.projection.project_node_payload` is renamed to
  `observe_payload`. Pass a `PayloadObservation` (for example
  `COMPACT_OBSERVATION`) instead of the previous `fallback_to_summary` keyword.
